channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TNVS driver killed during robbery incident in Taguig City
item.description: A transport network vehicle service (TNVS) driver was killed following a hold-up and stabbing incident in Taguig City late Saturday night. According to preliminary police reports, the victim was alleg...

item.title: Police start investigation on Edo LG Chairman over alleged financial irregularities
item.description: Di Nigeria Police Force don start investigation into alleged financial wahala wey dey Etsako East Local Government Area for Edo State. Dem don invite di acting Chairman make e show face on top March 2...

item.title: Unity and Discipline Sustain Tanzania’s Peace During Ramadan
item.description: Across Tanzania, the recently concluded fasting period of Ramadan has shown how collective responsibility and community cooperation can sustain peace and harmony. Citizens from different backgrounds o...

item.title: Zanzibar Football Set for Investment Boost Through League Valuation
item.description: The President of Zanzibar, Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi, has praised Tanzania Security Limited (TSL) for teaming up with the Zanzibar Football Federation (ZFF) to carry out a detailed valuation of the Zanzib...

item.title: Terrorists Abduct Nine Worshippers in Kwara Church During Sunday Service
item.description: In a shocking incident that has left the Oro-Ago community in Kwara State reeling, suspected terrorists stormed a church during a Sunday worship service, abducting nine worshippers. The attackers, hea...

item.title: East Sussex teacher struck off for inappropriate relationship with pupil
item.description: Benjamin Phelps, aged 31, has been permanently banned from teaching after a professional conduct panel found he had engaged in a highly inappropriate relationship with a boarding school pupil at St Be...

item.title: Rajasthan RBSE Class 10 Result Expected on March 23, 2026: How to Check and Download
item.description: The Rajasthan RBSE (Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education) is expected to announce the Class 10 results on March 23, 2026. Students who appeared for the exam can check their results online through th...

item.title: Students Seek Compensation for University Fees Paid During COVID-19 Online Learning
item.description: Almost 200,000 students who attended universities in England and Wales during the COVID-19 pandemic are pursuing financial compensation through the Student Group Claim. The legal action, led by law fi...

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau's Playoff Victory at Steyn City Draws Comparisons with Tiger Woods
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau's remarkable victory at the LIV event in Steyn City, where he claimed his second consecutive win on the first playoff hole, has drawn comparisons with Tiger Woods. Both DeChambeau an...

item.title: Renewed Fire at Karachi’s Gul Plaza Previously Damaged in January Blaze
item.description: A fresh fire broke out on Sunday at Karachi’s Gul Plaza, a shopping complex that had already suffered a deadly blaze earlier in January claiming 79 lives. According to fire brigade officials, smoke wa...

item.title: U.S. Troops Voice Increasing Concern Over Trump’s Iran War Strategy
item.description: As the U.S.-Israel war against Iran enters its fourth week, many American soldiers, sailors, and Marines are expressing deep unease about President Donald Trump’s escalating military campaign. Reports...

item.title: Fifteen Persons Detained in Ghaziabad for Suspected Espionage Activities
item.description: In Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh, fifteen individuals, including some minors, have been detained on suspicion of espionage. According to reports, the suspects allegedly filmed military sites, railway stati...

item.title: Australian government introduces framework for sustainable data centre and AI development
item.description: The Albanese government is rolling out a new national interest framework aimed at regulating the rapid expansion of data centres across Australia. Industry Minister Tim Ayres and Assistant Technology ...

item.title: Baba Ijesha alleges set-up by Princess Comedian leading to his rape conviction
item.description: In a recent interview on the Talk To B podcast, veteran Nigerian comic actor Olanrewaju 'Baba Ijesha' James has claimed that he was framed in a high-profile rape case. Baba Ijesha, who served a three-...

item.title: Saskatchewan forecasts $819 million budget deficit for 2026–27 fiscal year
item.description: Saskatchewan is projecting an $819 million deficit for the 2026–27 fiscal year, marking a significant shortfall as the province navigates economic and fiscal pressures. According to the provincial gov...

item.title: British Gas Chief Warns Energy Bills Likely to Rise Amid Prolonged High Oil Prices
item.description: Chris O'Shea, chief executive of Centrica, which owns British Gas, has warned that higher energy bills may be unavoidable if global oil prices remain elevated due to the ongoing conflict between Iran ...

item.title: Arsenal and Man City battle it out in Carabao Cup final for early silverware
item.description: Arsenal face Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final with both sides keen to claim their first major trophy of the season. The Gunners, led by Mikel Arteta, are hoping to secure their first major sil...

item.title: Analysts See Inquiry Report as Key to Strengthening Peace and Unity in Tanzania
item.description: Political analysts and academicians in Tanzania have expressed optimism about the upcoming report from the commission of inquiry investigating the events of October 29, 2025. The commission, chaired b...

item.title: NUPRC Enforces New Rule to Tackle Inactive Oil Blocks
item.description: The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) has officially begun enforcing the ‘drill-or-drop’ rule as part of the Petroleum Industry Act (PIA). This new provision mandates that oper...

item.title: Kimberley Mine Recovery: Two More Miners Found, Search Continues
item.description: Tragedy continues to unfold at the Ekapa Mine in Kimberley, Northern Cape, as two more bodies have been recovered, bringing the total recovered to three following a devastating mud rush incident last ...
